MOTION For Relief From Protective Order To Take Deposition Of Yano L. Rubinstein; Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Aug-10-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PHILLIP SELTZER OF RUBINSTEIN LAW, TEL. NO. 967-1970 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT SPECTOR & BENNETT'S MOTION For Relief From Protective Order To Take Deposition Of Yano L. Rubinstein - The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Kane as follows: Denied. Given the presumption against depositions of opposing counsel, the representation by Jones' counsel that Rubenstein will not be called as a witness, and the need for a showing of extreme good cause as set forth in the three factor test, Spector & Bennett has not has made a sufficient showing to overturn the protective order issued in February 2012. Monetary sanctions are denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =302/EG
